mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, it signals a structural deterioration in labor market tightness that reduces household income growth expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm synchronized expansion across consumer goods and durable asset markets, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization rebounds that force upward earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO surprises are negative in > 60% of post-shock recovery windows, or event study alpha is not significant at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index erodes real corporate profit margins and raises expectations for restrictive monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or inflation spikes consistently precede positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent, it confirms a robust expansionary phase that supports corporate revenue growth and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDP prints above 2.5% are negative in > 55% of observations, or predictive R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate healthy job creation without labor market slack, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by > 1% annually over a 5-year backtest, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when the federal funds rate remains below the annualized inflation rate, negative real yields sustain liquidity conditions that override nominal rate hikes, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize in > 60% of tightening periods where real rates are negative, or the spread variable loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample tests.
